idea.hook: How long would it REALLY take humanity to reach the closest star?
facts: Proxima Centauri, our nearest stellar neighbor, is 4.24 light-years away (approx. 40 TRILLION km).
Humanity's fastest spacecraft, the Parker Solar Probe, can reach speeds up to 690,000 km/h.
At this incredible speed, it would take you approximately 6,625 years to reach Proxima Centauri.
idea.payoff: Even at our peak technological speed, interstellar travel is an epic, multi-generational odyssey!
idea.title: Closest Star: 6,625 Year Trip?! 🤯🚀🌌

scenes.text: How long to reach the CLOSEST STAR?
scenes.visual: Dark space background with a subtle, faint distant star in the center.
A stylized Earth in one corner, with an arrow pointing to a distant, slightly larger star (Proxima Centauri) on the opposite side of the screen. Starfield background.

scenes.text: Fastest Ship: Parker Solar Probe @ 690,000 km/h
scenes.visual: A stylized animation of the Parker Solar Probe zipping across the screen, leaving a speed trail. Distant sun in the background.

scenes.text: At this speed... 6,625 YEARS to Proxima!
scenes.visual: A long, stylized timeline stretching across the screen, rapidly filling up with tick marks. A small, stylized probe icon moves slowly along it.
Earth and Proxima Centauri are both visible on screen, immensely far apart. A tiny, almost invisible line representing a probe slowly stretches between them, emphasizing the vast scale and time.
